title: Trachoma minuta
intro: Trachoma minuta is a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, commonly known as wild carrot or small wild carrot. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, wounds, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Trachoma minuta include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Trachoma minuta is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being added to topical creams and salves for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects.

The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal remedies, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
